image_style: Full notebook page, photographed, TWO diagrams + handwritten prose in one frame (uncropped)
notes: ONLY combinatorial-plausibility violation found across all testing (Part 1 n=23 + Part 2 n=6 + this = 30 total, 29/30 plausible). Grad-CAM showed attention spread across both diagrams and bleeding onto prose text. Likely cause: multi-object/cluttered frame outside training distribution (always single clean graph, no text). Not yet isolated from hand-drawn style itself -- see items 2 and 7 below for cropped/isolated comparisons.

image_style: Scanned academic paper figure (FIG. 1, planar/directed graph, arrows, varied line weight)
notes: Genuine scan, real print/scan artifacts. No warning fired -- estimated V<=100 and density well under dense threshold.

image_style: Clean vector diagram (z0/y0/x0/w0 lattice, generic n-notation)
notes: Computer-rendered but unfamiliar layout/labeling style vs. training renders. Not hand-drawn -- tests style robustness rather than real-world noise.

image_style: Digitally hand-drawn, rectangle nodes + text labels, directed edges (Twitter social network sketch)
notes: Very different visual grammar from training data (rectangles not circles, text-in-node). Highest estimated density of the batch.

image_style: Cartoon-icon nodes, directed edges (diagramming-tool demo, 'Social Network Bend=15%')
notes: Weakest test of core hypothesis (icon nodes are a stylistic edge case, not representative of hand-drawn/scanned real-world input); still combinatorially sane despite unfamiliar node rendering.

image_style: Clean vector, weighted directed network with numeric edge labels, Source/Sink + decoy gray nodes
notes: Largest failure by far: predicted V is >4.7x the training ceiling. Likely cause: dense overlapping numeric edge-labels read as extra node/texture signal. Still stayed combinatorially plausible despite the scale of the error -- notable given the severity.

image_style: Clean vector, circular numbered nodes (1-20), near-identical style to training renders
notes: Closest in visual grammar to training distribution. No warning fired; smallest overcount of the batch relative to true count (20 true vs 32 predicted, ~1.6x) -- consistent with 'error scales with visual unfamiliarity' pattern seen across items 2-6.
